name: Challah Bread
method: Combine lukewarm water, yeast, and 1 teaspoon sugar in a
small dish or measuring cup. Allow 5 minutes for the foam to
In the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the dough hook, whisk
together the flour, remaining 13 cup sugar, and salt. Mix on
medium speed with the yeast mixture, oil, and eggs until a
smooth dough forms and pulls away from the edge of the bowl,
about 10 to 15 minutes. The dough will remain slightly sticky.
Place dough in a greased basin and cover with plastic wrap. Let
rise until doubled, about 12 to 2 hours.
Preheat the oven to 350°F and line a large baking sheet with
parchment paper. Remove dough to a lightly floured board and
divide into three equal pieces. Roll each piece into a 20" long,
1" broad rope. Pinch the tops of the ropes together and tuck
them beneath the baking sheet. If required, gently wet your
hands to aid in pinching together.
Make a long rope out of the dough. Pinch the bottom ends of the
braid together and tuck them beneath the braid. Cover with a
kitchen towel and set aside for 1 hour, or until an indentation
remains when prodded.
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Brush the rising
dough with a mixture of egg, water, and sugar. If desired, top
with poppy seeds or sesame seeds.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until bread is golden. Before slicing,
allow it cool to room temperature.
